item.title: Canada and Norway move to capitalise on Iran war oil price surge
item.description: Nations position themselves as reliable suppliers in a ‘desperate’ world

item.title: Merck nears $6bn biotech deal to boost cancer drug pipeline
item.description: Terns Pharma stock has jumped since promising clinical data on its bone and blood cancer treatment last year

item.title: Pentagon appears to be ‘punishing’ Anthropic in violation of free speech, judge says
item.description: Jurist expresses scepticism about Trump administration’s position and calls defence department’s actions ‘troubling’

item.title: Investors snap up debt to finance Electronic Arts’ $55bn take-private
item.description: Bond deals tied to acquisition of video game maker show appetite for risky debt despite geopolitical tumult

item.title: OpenAI to end Disney deal and Sora video app
item.description: CEO Sam Altman is shifting AI start-up’s strategy to focus on its core products

item.title: UK backs switch to electric trucks and vans with £1bn funding
item.description: Government legally obliged to cut emissions under its commitment to reach net zero by 2050

item.title: Tech system failed to warn air traffic controllers of imminent crash at New York airport
item.description: Standard staffing was in place at LaGuardia, but officials have expressed reservations about overnight practices

item.title: FirstFT: Japanese megabank considers possible takeover of Jefferies
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Iran says ‘non-hostile’ ships can transit Strait of Hormuz and ADB launches emergency funding package

item.title: Charting the OpenAI ‘ecosystem’
item.description: Map of the problematique

item.title: Why are markets listening to Trump?
item.description: The price of chasing the news

item.title: Can Europe break free from US tech dominance? Submit your questions
item.description: Take part in a live Q&A with FT Innovation Editor John Thornhill and EU correspondent Barbara Moens, on Thursday March 26 at 1pm GMT

item.title: Jefferies’ M&A possibility brings forward the moment of reckoning
item.description: As interest rates have moved higher the US investment bank’s soured legacy deals and investments are coming to the fore

item.title: Iran says ‘non-hostile’ ships can transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Tehran tells IMO member nations that vessels must co-ordinate with it to pass through vital waterway

item.title: Daniel Křetínský ‘deeply sorry’ for state of Royal Mail
item.description: Czech billionaire denies claims postal workers are being asked to prioritise parcels over letters

item.title: Anglican clergy pension fund to vote against directors at NatWest, Santander and HSBC
item.description: Banks accused of backtracking on their climate promises ahead of annual meetings

item.title: Vets and their private equity owners get a light ride from UK competition watchdog
item.description: The six biggest veterinary groups control 60% of the £6.7bn market — and they can keep getting bigger

item.title: Indian cricket team Royal Challengers Bengaluru sold for $1.8bn
item.description: Diageo offloads IPL franchise to consortium that includes Aditya Birla and Times of India

item.title: VW to shift from cars to missile defence in deal with Israel’s Iron Dome maker
item.description: Venture between carmaker’s Osnabrück plant and Rafael Advanced Defence Systems part of plan to save 2,300 jobs

item.title: Arm launches own AI chip in high-stakes strategy shift
item.description: SoftBank-owned tech group secures Meta and OpenAI as first customers of its long-awaited new AI processor

item.title: Does Boaz Weinstein have Edinburgh Worldwide in a corner?
item.description: Watch this SpaceX

item.title: Janus Henderson bidding war escalates as Nelson Peltz and General Catalyst raise offer
item.description: All-cash bid increased by $3 a share to $52 following Victory Capital’s surprise swoop last month

item.title: UK housebuilder Bellway shares slump as CEO warns of softer market
item.description: Jason Honeyman says hopes of margin growth into 2027 ‘look quite optimistic’ in wake of Middle East conflict

item.title: Bank of London fined over faked documents
item.description: Fintech, where Peter Mandelson and Harvey Schwartz were board members, is given £2mn penalty by Bank of England

item.title: Government will not bail out wealthy households facing higher energy bills, says Reeves
item.description: Chancellor tells MPs that any support this winter will be targeted and will stay within fiscal rules

item.title: Crispin Odey admits grabbing former employee’s breasts without consent
item.description: Financier gives evidence for first time in his appeal against FCA ban and £1.8mn fine

item.title: War in Iran risks triggering cyber insurgency
item.description: Hackers are usually more interested in money than patriotism but this time might be different

item.title: Amazon’s Zoox targets paid robotaxi service as US race heats up
item.description: Driverless car group pushes forward with commercial rollout to catch up with rivals Waymo and Tesla

item.title: Hedge fund Millennium explores shifting Dubai staff to Jersey
item.description: Multi-manager firm weighs destinations after requests from staff to relocate

item.title: SEC questions ratings issued by agency behind private credit boom
item.description: Egan-Jones has come under scrutiny for its ratings on thousands of private loans relied on by insurers

item.title: Beauty group Puig surges 13% after disclosing Estée Lauder merger talks
item.description: Tie-up between Spanish and US groups would create $40bn fashion and beauty powerhouse
